display_name: Western Europe multi-era geometry + politics v1
do_not_claim: Paradox-grade completeness for any single era
legal historical frontiers — soft hints are illustrative

eras.notes: EU-leaning start with M15 WE period geometry (Cologne split, Flanders reshape, soft frontiers).
Victoria-leaning start with Belgian independence / Prussian Rhine soft geometry.
HOI-leaning interwar start with Maginot / Rhineland soft geometry.

breaking_changes: we-1444-v1 splits sample_de_rhineland into era_de_cologne + era_de_rhineland_residual.
we-1936-v1 replaces sample_de_rhineland with era_de_rhineland_1936 (different from 1444 split).
consumer_guidance: Pin game saves and mod data to era province IDs when consuming a period-geometry layer.
Use lineage.json / lineage.csv under eras/<era>/geometry/ to migrate scaffold-only packs.
Rebuild ownership tables after geometry revisions — politics do not rewrite polygons.
Read region_quality_matrix.json before claiming period geometry outside Western Europe.
do_not_claim: identical province IDs across all three eras
Paradox-grade HRE / German Confederation / interwar microborders
migration_notes.lineage_strategy: Each era-geometry pack emits its own lineage map (scaffold_province_id → era_province_id). Do not assume era IDs match across 1444/1836/1936. Cross-era analytics should join on scaffold_province_id.
migration_notes.summary: Scaffold province IDs are the stable cross-era join key. Era geometry packs may split or replace IDs within a version; use each pack's lineage.json to migrate. Politics are scenario overlays rebuilt per era.

priority_region.label: Western Europe
priority_region.notes: M16 multi-era pack covering 1444, 1836, and 1936 for the WE priority region.

region_quality_matrix.label: Europe elevated theaters (outside WE hard overrides)

source_notes: M16 multi-era packaging over M12–M15 era programs plus official-1936.
Geometry packs are priority-region scoped; politics remain curated overlays on open geodata.
